data.title: Japanese and South Korean stock markets closed lower.
data.description: The Nikkei 225 index closed down 632.54 points, or 1.06%, at 59,284.92 on Thursday, April 30. The South Korean KOSPI index closed down 93.03 points, or 1.39%, at 6,597.87 on Thursday, April 30. (Jinshi)

data.title: Ripple Expands in UAE with New Headquarters in Dubai
data.description: Ripple has inaugurated a new headquarters in the Dubai International Financial Centre, aiming to strengthen its presence in the Middle East and Africa. According to NS3.AI, the company stated that the new office will serve as a base to potentially double its workforce in the region. Ripple emphasized that the facility will support the growing demand for blockchain-based payment and custody services in the area.

data.title: Meta's European Stock Drops 7.2% Amid AI Spending and Legal Concerns
data.description: Meta's stock in Europe fell by 7.2% as the market expressed concerns over the company's spending on artificial intelligence and ongoing legal scrutiny. According to Jin10, investors are worried about the financial implications of Meta's investments in AI technologies and the potential impact of legal challenges on its operations. The decline reflects broader apprehensions about the sustainability of Meta's strategic initiatives in the face of regulatory pressures.

data.title: BitGo Transfers 312 BTC to New Wallets
data.description: Two newly created wallets have received a total of 312 BTC from BitGo over the past 24 hours, valued at $23.62 million. According to NS3.AI, this transfer highlights ongoing activity in the cryptocurrency market, as significant amounts of Bitcoin are moved between wallets. The reasons behind these transfers remain unclear, but they could be related to strategic repositioning or security measures.

data.title: Paxos Expands Crypto Brokerage Platform to Support BONK
data.description:
Blockchain infrastructure company Paxos, regulated in multiple countries, has announced that its Crypto Brokerage platform now officially supports BONK. According to ChainCatcher, Paxos holds compliance licenses from authorities such as the U.S. OCC, Finland's FIN-FSA, and Singapore's MAS, enabling its partners, including traditional financial institutions, to offer secure and compliant BONK trading and custody services.

Paxos highlighted that BONK has nearly 1 million holders and over 400 integrations within the Solana ecosystem, indicating a vibrant community base. This development marks BONK's transition from a community-driven meme coin to mainstream financial channels. Currently, leveraging the same compliance platform trusted by institutions like PayPal, Mastercard, and Venmo, more than 500 million users worldwide can purchase BONK through major retail trading platforms and exchanges.

data.title: South Korea's Virtual Asset Tax Plan Sparks Fairness Debate
data.description: South Korea's decision to implement a tax on virtual assets next year has reignited discussions on fairness following the repeal of the financial investment income tax. According to NS3.AI, the opposition People Power Party has announced plans to reassess the tax system. Meanwhile, the National Tax Service has indicated that the existing law is being prepared to tax income generated from 2027, with initial filings expected in 2028.

data.title: Trust Wallet has officially integrated Hyperliquid, allowing users to trade crypto assets, commodities, and precious metals perpetual contracts in one place within the app.
data.description: Trust Wallet has announced its official integration with Hyperliquid, an on-chain perpetual contract trading platform, allowing users to trade perpetual contracts directly within the app. Users will gain access to deeper liquidity, narrower spreads, and a diversified trading market, with spot, futures, and RWA trading all available in one place. Hyperliquid is currently the most liquid decentralized exchange, with a cumulative trading volume exceeding $4 trillion, supporting perpetual contract trading for crypto assets and real-world assets (RWAs) such as oil and precious metals. Following this integration, Trust Wallet users can directly participate in all Hyperliquid market trading by opening the app.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Kite Launches Mainnet for AI Agent Economy Payment Infrastructure
data.description:
Kite has officially launched its mainnet, providing a payment infrastructure tailored for the AI Agent economy. According to Foresight News, during the initial phase, Kite Treasury will cover network fees, allowing users to bypass Gas fees. The company has also introduced the Kite Agent Passport, which serves as an authorization and payment control layer for agents. Users can utilize a Passkey to sign controlled sessions, which include limits on single transactions, total budgets, validity periods, and permitted assets and payment methods. Agents can autonomously execute tasks within these constraints without requiring access to users' bank accounts, credit cards, or private keys. All activities are logged for auditing purposes.

Kite employs a 'three-layer integrated' architecture. The foundational settlement layer is an EVM-compatible L1 that natively supports stablecoin settlements, designed for high-frequency, low-value, agent-driven payment scenarios. The middle layer, the core Agent service, is supported by the Passport, which handles identity, delegation, and risk control. The top layer, the Agent interface, allows mainstream agents to connect directly.

The Kite team emphasizes that they are not focused on Total Value Locked (TVL) but will measure progress through total transaction numbers, monthly active Passport numbers, and total settlement amounts. Currently, users can register accounts, log agents, and initiate controlled delegation authorizations. The first batch of agents can automatically complete tasks such as online shopping, paid API calls, and multi-step tasks across services.

data.title: Japan's Food and Beverage Prices Set to Rise Amid Naphtha Shortage
data.description: Japan's food and beverage prices are expected to increase as early as this summer due to a shortage of naphtha, a key raw material for plastic packaging, according to a report by Teikoku Databank. The ongoing disruption of energy flows through the Strait of Hormuz has reduced the supply of naphtha, prompting suppliers of polypropylene and polyethylene packaging to demand immediate price hikes. Small and medium-sized food manufacturers have already begun to feel the impact. According to Jin10, the report released on Thursday covers food and beverage categories that have already experienced or are expected to experience price increases by September, with confectionery products, including chocolate, anticipated to be most affected. In March, Japan's core consumer prices, excluding fresh food, rose by 1.8% year-on-year, surpassing economists' median expectations and the previous month's 1.6% increase. The index excluding fresh food and energy, considered an important measure of underlying inflation by the Bank of Japan, rose by 2.4%, remaining above the 2% target.

data.title: Two newly created wallets received 312 BTC, worth $23.62 million, from BitGo within 24 hours.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens, in the past 24 hours, two newly created wallets received 312 BTC from BitGo, worth $23.62 million.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Glencore Reports First Quarter Production Figures for Lead, Gold, Silver, and Cobalt
data.description: Glencore has released its production figures for the first quarter, detailing output across several key commodities. According to Jin10, the company produced 41,200 tons of lead, 68,000 ounces of gold, 4.87 million ounces of silver, and 5,800 tons of cobalt during this period. These figures provide insight into Glencore's operational performance and resource extraction capabilities.

data.title: Germany's March Import Price Index Surpasses Expectations
data.description: Germany's import price index for March showed a monthly increase of 3.6%, exceeding the anticipated 3% and significantly higher than the previous month's 0.3%. According to Jin10, this data reflects a notable rise in import costs, which could impact economic forecasts and trade dynamics.

data.title: Glencore's First Quarter Production Meets Expectations
data.description: Glencore reported that its production for the first quarter was largely in line with expectations. According to Jin10, the company stated that its output across various commodities met the anticipated levels for the period. Glencore's performance in the first quarter reflects its operational stability and adherence to projected production targets.

data.title: Steak 'n Shake Executive Integrates Bitcoin into Compensation Plan
data.description:
Steak 'n Shake executive Michael Boes announced at the 'Bitcoin 2026' conference that he has incorporated Bitcoin into his personal executive compensation plan. According to Foresight News, Boes hopes this move will serve as a reference for compensation models within the industry.

Previously, Steak 'n Shake implemented Bitcoin payments via the Lightning Network across 393 U.S. locations in May 2025, with all Bitcoin receipts directly contributing to the company's Bitcoin reserves. Since March 1, employees have been receiving an additional 21 cents in Bitcoin for every hour worked.

data.title: SEC Approves Rule Change for iShares Bitcoin Trust Options
data.description:
The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has recently approved a rule change proposal submitted by the Nasdaq ISE exchange, according to ChainCatcher. The proposal, identified as SR-ISE-2025-26 and amended by the fifth amendment, significantly increases the position and exercise limits for iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) options from 250,000 contracts to 1 million contracts.

The SEC stated in its approval order that, after reviewing the data and analysis provided by the exchange, the new limits comply with Section 6(b)(5) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934. This section aims to prevent fraudulent and manipulative practices while protecting investors and the public interest.

data.title: Hong Kong Commercial Aerospace Stocks Rise Amid Market Volatility
data.description: Hong Kong's commercial aerospace sector experienced a notable uptick, with several key stocks showing significant gains. According to Jin10, Goldwind Technology (02208.HK) surged over 6%, while Asia Pacific Satellite (01045.HK) and Junda Co., Ltd. (02865.HK) also saw increases. The market movement reflects growing investor interest in the aerospace industry.

data.title: Polymarket Introduces New WTI Crude Oil Price Predictions for May
data.description: Polymarket has launched new predictions for the price of WTI crude oil in May, offering a range of price brackets from $20 to $150. According to Odaily, the settlement rules specify that if any one-minute candlestick closing price of the nearest WTI crude oil futures contract reaches or exceeds the predicted price, the market outcome will be considered 'Yes.' For June, the settlement requires the closing price on the trading day to be higher than the predicted price to be deemed 'Yes.'

data.title: Volkswagen CFO Reports Operating Profit Margin Below Expectations
data.description: Volkswagen Group's Chief Financial Officer stated that despite some progress, the operating profit margin remains at 4.3%, which is significantly below expectations, even before accounting for special project impacts. According to Jin10, this figure highlights the challenges the company faces in meeting its financial targets.

data.title: AI Assistants Decline as Founders Return to Hardware
data.description: AI TRENDS | The trend of AI assistants is waning as hardware makes a comeback, with founders returning to their former companies. According to PANews, the shift marks a significant change in focus within the tech industry. As AI assistants lose prominence, the emphasis is moving back to hardware development, prompting founders to revisit their roots and re-engage with their original enterprises. This development highlights a notable transition in the priorities of tech innovators.

data.title: Polymarket has updated its May crude oil price forecast, which differs from the June forecast and is based on the closing price one minute in advance.
data.description: Odaily Seer's monitoring shows that Polymarket has released new May price forecasts for WTI crude oil, including multiple levels ranging from $20 to $150. The settlement rule is that if the highest closing price of any 1-minute candlestick chart for the near-month WTI crude oil futures contract reaches or exceeds the predicted price, the market settlement result is "Yes". The June settlement rule requires the settlement price on the trading day to be higher than the predicted price for it to be considered "Yes". Odaily Seer continuously monitors the forecast market, seeing changes before pricing.
